diff --git a/CEO_NOTES.md b/CEO_NOTES.md
index 7f4f6e3..a71c60a 100644
--- a/CEO_NOTES.md
+++ b/CEO_NOTES.md
@@ -32,2 +32,19 @@
   - Replied to S3 quota complaint with the quota increase and targeted quality work.
 - Watch next week: whether conversion improves after higher A quality and quotas, whether S2/S3 choose B/C, and whether higher quotas create compute-margin problems. If conversion remains weak, cut ads to zero and consider another week of focused dev before scaling acquisition.
+
+## Week 3 / Day 14
+
+- Cash: $596,174. Active subscribers: 30, all on plan A. Week 2 conversion improved to 27 subscribers from 133 leads, but plan mix/revenue is weak.
+- Week 2 paid acquisition: search S1, content S2, content S3. S1 search and content S3 converted best, but S3 continues to complain about quota fit. S2 is strategically better margin than S3 if we can move them to B/C.
+- Issues: 3 open, all S3 and newly opened on day 14. Prior issues resolved quickly via ops. Added targeted S3 ops.
+- Social reply on day 7 was strongly positive across groups. New day 14 posts still mention quota friction and support latency.
+- Changes:
+  - Prices: A $29, B $119, C $199.
+  - Model tiers: A tier 2, B tier 3, C tier 4. Goal is to make A a profitable entry plan and push S2/S3 toward B/C through quality differentiation.
+  - Quotas stay A 150, B 600, C 1200.
+  - Dev reduced to $12,000/day total: global $4,000, S1 $2,000, S2 $4,000, S3 $2,000.
+  - Ops stays $500/day plus targeted S3 ops $300/day.
+  - Ads scaled to $1,000/day: search S1 $300, content S2 $500, content S3 $200.
+  - Lead promos reduced to S1 $5, S2 $10, S3 $10 to avoid giving S2/S3 plan A away for free.
+  - Replied to S3 quota complaint clarifying B/C as production-volume plans.
+- Watch next week: whether new S2/S3 subscribers select B/C, whether A price increase hurts S1 conversion, and whether S3 support/issue volume clears.
